content: No, poverty is not an inevitable part of every social structure. While poverty has existed in many societies throughout history, it is not a necessary or inherent feature of human societies. In fact, some countries, such as Norway and Denmark, have achieved very low poverty rates through a combination of social welfare policies, economic equality, and strong social safety nets.
raw_content: No, poverty is not an inevitable part of every social structure. While poverty has existed in many societies throughout history, it is not a necessary or inherent feature of human societies. In fact, some countries, such as Norway and Denmark, have achieved very low poverty rates through a combination of social welfare policies, economic equality, and strong social safety nets.
